About: It is about the cultural unity of Britons.
It is about the definition of Britishness that may include people of English, Scottish, Welsh, and Northern Irish descent.
Affects: It affects the political union that is the United Kingdom.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with British unionism.
It is associated with the Ulster Unionist Party.
It is associated with the Democratic Unionist Party.
It is associated with the Scottish Unionist Party.
It is associated with the Conservative Party.
It is associated with the Labour Party.
It is associated with the Liberal Democrat Party.
It is associated with the Reform UK party.
It is associated with the British National Party.
It is associated with other nativist nationalist movements.
HasA: It has been a civic nationalism in its moderate form.
It has a sense of dignity.
It has been described as xenophobic at times.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized as a powerful but ambivalent force in British politics.
It is characterized by emphasis on both cohesion and diversity of the people of the United Kingdom, its dependencies, and its former colonies in its moderate form.
It is characterized by concerns relating to immigration in its nativist form.
It is characterized by overt aggression in other nationalist movements.
It is characterized by support for the monarchy.
It is characterized by support for the houses of parliament.
It is characterized by support for the judiciary.
It is characterized by support for other elements of the institutional status quo.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Anglo-Irish calls for unity with Britain.
IsA: It is a civic nationalism in its moderate form.
It is an anti-immigrant nativist nationalism.
It is a popular patriotic nationalism.
It is a civic nationalist movement.
KnownFor: It is known for promoting the cultural unity of Britons.
It is known for seeking to uphold the political union that is the United Kingdom.
It is known for strengthening the links between the countries of the United Kingdom.
It is known for its unifying identity descending from the ancient Britons.
It is known for emphasizing both cohesion and diversity of the people of the United Kingdom, its dependencies, and its former colonies.
It is known for arising based on concerns relating to immigration.
It is known for lacking the overt aggression seen in other nationalist movements.
It is known for being marked by confidence, a sense of dignity, and some arrogance.
It is known for focusing less on a perceived inherent difference and more on the historic superiority of its liberal traditions.
It is known for its perceived capacity for international leadership.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Industrial Revolution.
It occurred during the mid-18th century.
It occurred during the Second World War.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United Kingdom.
It occurred in the Devolved Nations.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the ancient Britons.
It originated in the island of Great Britain.
PartOf: It is part of British politics.
It is part of the United Kingdom.
It is part of the institutional status quo.
UsedFor: It is used for promoting the cultural unity of Britons.
It is used for upholding the political union that is the United Kingdom.
It is used for strengthening the links between the countries of the United Kingdom.
It is used for promoting British nationalism as a progressive cause.
UsedIn: It is used in British politics.
It is used in the United Kingdom.
It is used in the Devolved Nations.
It is used in Northern Ireland.
UsedTo: It is used to promote the cultural unity of Britons.
It is used to uphold the political union that is the United Kingdom.
It is used to strengthen the links between the countries of the United Kingdom.
It is used to promote British nationalism as a progressive cause.
